title: picris scabra subsp. abyssinica
intro: Picris scabra subsp. abyssinica, also known as Abyssinian picris, is a subspecies of the Picris scabra plant, which belongs to the family Compositae and is native to certain regions of Ethiopia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which contribute to its traditional use in treating various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Picris scabra subsp. abyssinica include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and combat infections, making it a valuable resource in folk medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing its leaves or roots in hot water to create a tea or by using it as a topical application to treat skin irritations.
